customer_addr:

Nashotah, WI 53058
site_name: Oceans of Fun Panama City Beach

citations.narrative:
While the facility is frequently in communication with their attending veterinarian and there are no current animal health
concerns, visual examination of the facility's three sea lions by the attending veterinarian is not occurring on a semiannual
basis. At the time of inspection, these sea lions were last examined on June 3, 2025, and the next veterinary visit is
scheduled for February 2026. This is an interval of eight months and does not meet the requirement for marine mammals
to be visually examined every six months. When the APHIS Official brought this to the attention of the facility, they then
scheduled an earlier veterinary visit for later that week.
Visual exams performed under the direction of the attending veterinarian provide a real-time assessment of each marine
mammals’ health condition and status. Conducting these in-person visual exams at consistent intervals ensures that the
attending veterinarian is able to identify any issues or concerns in a timely manner. All marine mammals must be visually
examined by the attending veterinarian at least semiannually and must be physically examined under the supervision of
and when determined to be necessary by the attending veterinarian.
To be corrected from this day, January 16, 2026, forward.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
3.110(f)
